The history of radio broadcasting. Radio began as a dream of distance. In the final years of the 19th century, Gugliel Mohmarkoni demonstrated that electromagnetic waves could carry signals across open air without wires, first across a garden, then a harbor, and eventually the Atlantic. Those early spark-app transmitters produced only dots and dashes, yet they proved a principle that would reshape public life. Information could travel at the speed of light, and anyone with a receiver could liste
